message:
fix(services-setup): Fix control flow bug where main() was never called

The maybe_run_cli_action function always returns 0, so the original code:
  if maybe_run_cli_action "$@"; then exit 0; fi
would always exit without running main().

This fix properly checks if SERVICES_SETUP_ACTION was set by the arg
parser, and only then runs the CLI action. Otherwise it falls through
to the interactive wizard via main().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1252,8 +1252,10 @@ Happy coding!"
 
 # Run main if not sourced
 if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]; then
-    if maybe_run_cli_action "$@"; then
-        exit 0
+    maybe_run_cli_action "$@"
+    if [[ -n "${SERVICES_SETUP_ACTION:-}" ]]; then
+        run_cli_action
+        exit $?
     fi
     main "$@"
 fi
